summary: Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bomb attacks on Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Donetsk regions, while Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV incursions across northern and eastern fronts. Russian forces continued offensive actions in Konstantinovka, seizing hundreds of buildings and inflicting heavy Ukrainian losses. Ukrainian authorities confirmed a drone strike on civilian vessels in the Black Sea, causing casualties.
key_points: Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
Three Tu-22M3 bombers took off from Olenya airfield, with four bombers airborne in total (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67907).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV attacks on civilian ships in the Black Sea, killing one crew member and injuring several others (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
Russian forces liberated 627 buildings in Konstantinovka and seized additional strongholds in Krasy Liman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down two UAVs over Sevastopol and the northern side of the Zaporozhye region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
A fuel tanker parking lot was struck in southern Odessa region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31017).
Ukrainian UAVs targeted logistics facilities in Zaporizhzhia, causing eight injuries and a fire (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Russian drone attacks hit civilian infrastructure in southern Odesa, resulting in one death and multiple injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82045).

sections.executive_summary: Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bomb attacks on Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk regions, intensifying pressure on Ukrainian defenses. Concurrently, Russian ground forces advanced in Konstantinovka, capturing hundreds of buildings and inflicting significant Ukrainian casualties. Ukrainian forces reported UAV strikes on civilian maritime targets in the Black Sea, highlighting the expanding scope of the conflict.

points: Ukrainian air defenses downed two UAVs over Sevastopol and the northern side of the Zaporozhye region, demonstrating continued air defense activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
A drone strike on civilian vessels in the Black Sea killed one crew member and injured several others, underscoring vulnerability of maritime traffic (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
Ukrainian UAVs struck a logistics operator building in Zaporizhzhia, injuring eight people, including a police officer, and causing a fire (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
A fuel tanker parking lot in southern Odessa region was hit, indicating Russian targeting of logistical assets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31017).
Ukrainian forces reported multiple guided bomb attacks on their territory, reflecting heightened Russian aerial pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
Three Tu-22M3 bombers launched from Olenya airfield, with a total of four bombers airborne, indicating a significant strike capability deployment (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67907).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aerial bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk regions, expanding the use of precision munitions (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
Russian ground forces liberated 627 buildings in Konstantinovka and seized 37 strongholds in Krasy Liman, showing continued offensive momentum (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Russian forces reported striking a fuel tanker parking lot in the southern Odessa region, targeting fuel logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31017).
Russian UAV activity was noted over the Rostov region, Crimea, Sevastopol, Melitopol and Kirillovka, with air defenses actively engaging (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Guided aerial bomb attacks were reported in the Dnipropetrovsk region, indicating Russian focus on eastern industrial areas (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
Ukrainian forces noted UAV activity in the Sumy direction, with infantry battles reported in Bachivsk and surrounding villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Russian GRU "North" continued infantry battles in the Kharkiv direction, including villages Losevka and Ukrainka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
In the eastern Zaporozhye region, Russian GRU "East" units penetrated towards Lyubitskoe and Zarnitsa, showing deep operational pushes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Ukrainian UAV attacks on logistics facilities in Zaporizhzhia caused casualties and fire, affecting eastern supply lines (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Two UAVs were shot down over Sevastopol and the northern side of the Zaporozhye region, reflecting active air defense in the north (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Russian UAV attacks hit a village in the Kursk region (Stepankovka), injuring a civilian, indicating cross-border drone activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV incursions from Chernihiv towards Kyiv region (Vishhorod district) (Source: @kpszsu/65577).
Guided bomb attacks were also reported in Donetsk region, part of the northern front's contested area (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
Russian air defense activity was noted over the Rostov region and Crimea, suggesting broader northern air operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
A drone strike on civilian infrastructure in southern Odesa resulted in one death and multiple injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82045).
Russian forces struck a fuel tanker parking lot in the southern Odessa region, targeting fuel supplies (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31017).
Ukrainian UAV attacks on logistics facilities in Zaporizhzhia caused eight injuries and a fire, affecting southern supply routes (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Guided aerial bomb attacks were reported in the Zaporizhzhia region, a key southern operational area (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
Russian tactical aviation conducted bomb runs over the Donetsk region, indicating southern front pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
Three Tu-22M3 bombers took off from Olenya airfield, with four bombers airborne, showing a significant strategic bomber deployment (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67907).
Russian tactical aviation used guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk regions, expanding precision strike usage (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down two UAVs over Sevastopol and northern Zaporozhye, demonstrating active interception (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Ukrainian UAVs targeted logistics sites in Zaporizhzhia, causing casualties and fire, indicating offensive drone operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Russian UAV activity was reported over the Rostov region, Crimea, Melitopol and Kirillovka, with Ukrainian defenses responding (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).

key_developments: Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bomb attacks on Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk regions, intensifying aerial pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
Three Tu-22M3 bombers departed Olenya airfield, with four bombers airborne, indicating a major bomber sortie (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67907).
Ukrainian air defenses downed two UAVs over Sevastopol and northern Zaporozhye, showing effective air defense (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Russian ground forces liberated 627 buildings in Konstantinovka and seized strongholds in Krasy Liman, reflecting continued offensive gains (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
A drone strike on civilian vessels in the Black Sea killed one crew member and injured several, highlighting maritime threats (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
Ukrainian UAV attacks on a logistics facility in Zaporizhzhia injured eight and caused a fire, impacting supply lines (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
A fuel tanker parking lot in southern Odessa was hit, targeting fuel logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31017).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are escalating aerial and ground operations, employing guided bombs and strategic bombers to pressure key Ukrainian regions while advancing on the ground in Konstantinovka and Krasy Liman. Ukrainian air defenses remain active, successfully intercepting UAVs, but civilian infrastructure continues to suffer from drone and missile strikes, especially in maritime and southern zones. The combined effect increases operational risk for Ukrainian logistics and civilian populations across multiple fronts.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian command is reinforcing air defense assets around strategic ports and southern logistics hubs, while also conducting counter‑UAV operations against Russian drone incursions. Regional authorities in Odessa and Zaporizhzhia are coordinating emergency response to civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. International monitoring of maritime incidents in the Black Sea is expected to intensify, given recent civilian ship attacks.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of Russian bomber sortie patterns from Olenya is critical for early warning. UAV launch sites in Chernihiv and Sumy regions suggest a shift toward northern and eastern aerial harassment. Fuel logistics in southern Odessa remain a high‑value target, requiring enhanced protection. The high volume of guided bomb usage indicates increased Russian reliance on precision strikes, necessitating updated Ukrainian counter‑measures.
